Item 1. Business
General
Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet. We are integrating our product portfolios across networking, security, collaboration, applications and the cloud to create highly secure, intelligent platforms for our customers' digital businesses. These platforms are designed to help our customers manage more users, devices and things connecting to their networks. This will enable us to provide customers with a highly secure, intelligent platform for their digital business.
We conduct our business globally and manage our business by geography. Our business is organized into the following three geographic segments: Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC).
Our products and technologies are grouped into the following categories: Secure, Agile Networks; Internet for the Future; Collaboration; End-to-End Security; Optimized Application Experiences; and Other Products. In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service offerings, including technical support services and advanced services. Increasingly, we are delivering our technologies through software and services. Our custom
…[truncated 20799 chars]…
ross our security portfolio focused on cloud-based offerings, AI-driven threat detection and end-to-end security architectures. Additionally, we continue to invest in expanding our SASE architecture by delivering combined network and security functionality in a single cloud-native service.
5
Table of Contents
Optimized Application Experiences
The Optimized Application Experiences product category consists of our full stack observability and network assurance offerings. Our full stack observability offerings are designed to bring together and provide end-to-end visibility of our customer's environments across applications, networks, multi-cloud infrastructures and the Internet, to help deliver full stack observability for modern environments and drive relevant real-time insights. Our Intersight Infrastructure Services, a cloud-delivered SaaS offering, provides lifecycle operations management, automation, orchestration, and monitoring capabilities for customers' infrastructure deployments from Data Center to the Edge. Our monitoring and analytics offering, AppDynamics, monitors performance across different application-related domains. Our network assurance offering, ThousandEyes, is a network intelligence platform that provides in-depth visibility into network and Internet performance. It enables organizations to see, understand, and improve every digital experience and assure seamless connectivity for their modern digital environments.
Services
In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service and support options for our customers. Our overall service and support offerings are combined into one organization, Customer Experience, that is responsible for the end-to-end customer experience.
Our support and maintenance services help our customers ensure their products operate efficiently, remain available, and benefit from the most up-to-date system and application software. These services help customers protect their network investments, manage risk, and minimize downtime for systems running mission-critical applications.
We also provide comprehensive advisory services that are focused on responsive, preventive, and consultative support of our technologies for specific networking needs. We are investing in and expanding advisory services in the areas of software, cloud, security, and analytics, which reflects our strategy of selling customer outcomes. We are focused on three priorities: utilizing technology advisory services to drive higher product and services; assessment and migration services providing the tools, expertise and methodologies to enable our customers to migrate to new technology platforms; and providing optimization services aligned with customers' business expectations.
Customers and Markets
Many factors influence the IT, collaboration, and networking requirements of our customers. These include the size of the organization, number and types of technology systems, geographic location, and business applications deployed throughout the customer's network. Our customer base is not limited to any specific industry, geography, or market segment. Our customers primarily operate in the following markets: enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector.
Enterprise
Enterprise businesses are large regional, national, or global organizations with multiple locations or branch offices. Many enterprise businesses have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multivendor environment. We offer service and support packages, financing, and managed network services, primarily through our service provider partners. We sell these products through a network of third-party application and technology vendors and channel partners, as well as selling directly to these customers.
Commercial
The commercial market represents larger, or midmarket and small businesses. We sell to our midmarket customers through a combination of our direct sales force and channel partners. These customers typically require the latest advanced technologies that our enterprise customers demand, but with less complexity. Small businesses require information technologies and communications products that are easy to configure, install, and maintain. We sell to these smaller organizations within the commercial market primarily through channel partners.
Service Providers
Service providers offer data, voice, video, and mobile/wireless services to businesses, governments, utilities, and consumers worldwide. The service provider market includes regional, national, and international wireline carriers, webscale operators as well as Internet, cable, and wireless providers. We also include media, broadcast, and content providers within our service provider market, as the lines in the telecommunications industry continue to blur between traditional network-based, content-based and application-based services. Service providers use a variety of our products and services for their own networks. In addition, many service providers use Cisco data center, virtualization, and collaboration technologies to offer managed or Internet-based services to their business customers. Compared with other customers, service providers are more likely to require network design, deployment, and support services because of the greater scale and higher complexity of their networks, whose requirements are addressed, we believe, by our architectural approach.
6
Table of Contents
Public Sector
The public sector market includes federal, state and local governments, as well as educational institution customers. Many public sector customers have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multi-vendor environment. We sell to public sector customers through a network of third-party application and technology vendors, channel partners, as well as through direct sales.
Sales Overview
As of the end of fiscal 2023, our worldwide sales and marketing functions consisted of approximately 26,000 employees, including managers, sales representatives, and technical support personnel. We sell our products and services both directly and indirectly through a variety of channels with support from our salesforce. A substantial portion of our products and services is sold indirectly through channel partners, and the remainder is sold through direct sales. Channel partners include systems integrators, service providers, other third-party resellers, and distributors.
Systems integrators and service providers typically sell directly to end users and often provide system installation, technical support, professional services, and other support services in addition to network equipment sales. Systems integrators also typically integrate our products into an overall solution. Some service providers are also systems integrators.
Distributors may hold inventory and sell to systems integrators, service providers, and other third-party resellers. We refer to sales through distributors as our two-tier system of sales to the end customer. Revenue from two-tier distributors is recognized based on a sell-in method. These distributors may be given business terms that allow them to return a limited portion of inventory, receive credits for changes in selling prices, receive certain rebates, and participate in various cooperative marketing programs.
For information regarding risks related to our sales channels, see "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factors entitled "Disruption of or changes in our distribution model could harm our sales and margins" and "Inventory management relating to our sales to our two-tier distribution channel is complex, and excess inventory may harm our gross margins."

CSCO.quantitative_report:
**CSCO Quantitative Report (Evidence to 2024-01-01)**

**1. Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Q1 FY24 (2023-10-28) $14.67B, down 3.5% QoQ but up 7.6% YoY. 8-quarter trend: from $12.72B (Q2 FY22) to $14.67B, peak at $15.20B (Q4 FY23).
- **Earnings**: Q1 FY24 EPS diluted $0.89, down 7.3% QoQ but up 36.9% YoY. 8-quarter trend: from $0.71 to $0.89, peak $0.96 (Q4 FY23).
- **Margins**: Gross margin 65.2% (9.56B/14.67B), up from 61.3% YoY. Operating margin 29.2% (4.28B/14.67B), up from 26.0% YoY. Net margin 24.8% (3.64B/14.67B), up from 19.6% YoY.
- **Cash Flow**: No data supplied.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets $98.78B, total equity $45.21B, total debt $7.70B. Debt reduced from $8.90B (Q1 FY23). Equity grew from $40.27B over same period.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Strong YoY EPS growth (+36.9%) with expanding margins. QoQ EPS decline (-7.3%) raises trajectory questions. No cash flow data prevents conversion assessment.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Debt-to-equity 0.17 (7.70/45.21), down from 0.22 a year prior. Total equity covers 84.4% of total liabilities (53.57B). Liquidity/reserves not quantified without cash flow data.

**3. Valuation vs. Own History**
- **PE Diluted**: 15.2 (2023-12-29), within 2023 range 14.4–20.7, currently at the lower end. Historical 12-point average PE ~17.6.
- **Price-to-Book**: 4.567, within 2023 range 4.3–5.6.
- **Earnings Yield**: 6.6% (1/PE).
- **Context**: Valuation compressed as PE declined from ~20 in August 2023 despite earnings growth. Current PE is 13% below the period's midpoint (~17.55).

**4. Price & Returns vs. Fundamentals**
- **Price**: $50.52 (2023-12-29), within 52-week range $45.55–$58.19.
- **Quarterly Return**: -6.0% (vs. Q1 FY24 YoY EPS +36.9%).
- **Annual Return**: +7.3% (vs. YoY EPS growth +36.9% and revenue growth +7.6%).
- **Path**: Price rose 9.3% in Q1 2023, then oscillated, ending the year +7.3% while fundamentals strengthened.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside**: 
    - PE reversion to 2023 midpoint (~17.55) would imply ~15% price appreciation from $50.52 (holding EPS constant at $0.89 quarterly, ~$3.56 annualized).
    - Continued YoY earnings growth (36.9% latest) and margin expansion (gross margin +390 bps YoY).
    - Debt reduction and equity growth strengthening balance sheet.
- **Downside**:
    - QoQ revenue (-3.5%) and EPS (-7.3%) declines may signal slowing momentum.
    - Lack of cash flow data obscures true earnings quality and capital return capacity.
    - If growth stalls, low PE may reflect stagnation rather than value.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward**
- **Reward**: Low relative valuation (PE 15.2 vs. history), strong YoY fundamental growth, and balance-sheet strength provide upside if growth sustains and multiples normalize.
- **Risk**: Recent quarterly softness may persist; earnings quality unverifiable without cash flow. Price has underperformed fundamentals over the past year.
- **Conclusion**: Quantitatively, the setup favors conditional upside if QoQ trends stabilize, but the absence of cash flow data and the recent quarterly decline warrant caution. Valuation is attractive relative to history, but not undeniably cheap given the trajectory ambiguity.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment Report: Cisco Systems (CSCO)
**Report Date:** 2024-01-01 (Assessment based on evidence up to 2023-12-31)

#### 1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative
As confirmed in its 2023-09-07 10-K filing, Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet. The company is integrating portfolios across networking, security, collaboration, and applications to create secure, intelligent platforms for digital businesses. Its core product categories are:
*   **Secure, Agile Networks:** Core enterprise networking hardware/software (e.g., Catalyst switches, SD-Access).
*   **Internet for the Future:** Carrier and web-scale routing, silicon (Cisco Silicon One), and optics.
*   **Collaboration:** The Webex suite for hybrid work, including meetings, calling, and devices.
*   **End-to-End Security:** A portfolio focusing on cloud-based and AI-driven security architectures (SASE, Zero Trust).
*   **Optimized Application Experiences:** Full stack observability (ThousandEyes, AppDynamics, Intersight).
*   **Services:** Technical support, advanced services, and advisory/customer experience services in software, cloud, and security.

The company's stated strategy is to "securely connect everything," with announced customer priorities to transform infrastructure, secure the enterprise, power hybrid work, reimagine applications, and drive toward sustainability. A key aspect of its strategy is transforming its business model toward increasing software, subscriptions, and as-a-service offerings (e.g., Cisco Plus).

#### 2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)
*   **Q1 FY2024 Results (2023-11-15):** Cisco reported Q1 FY2024 earnings, with management noting "record results on both revenue and profitability." The quarter showed strong operating leverage and double-digit year-over-year growth in software revenue, product annualized recurring revenue (ARR), and remaining performance obligations (RPO).
*   **Strategic Catalyst - Splunk Acquisition (2023-12-04):** Analyst commentary indicates Cisco's pending acquisition of Splunk is expected to "significantly expand its software revenue," aligning with industry trends. Market sentiment, per this source, is optimistic on the long-term benefits.
*   **Legal Outcome - Centripetal Patent Case (2023-12-12 & 2023-12-13):** Following a remand, a U.S. District Court ruled that Cisco did not infringe Centripetal Networks' patents, allowing Cisco to dodge a previously awarded $755 million damages penalty.
*   **Legal Settlement - False Claims (2023-12-11):** Cisco paid $8.6 million to settle a dispute over alleged vulnerabilities in its products, described as the first False Claims Act case of its kind for a security company.
*   **Market Perception (Mixed, 2023-12-22 & 2023-12-29):** On 2023-12-22, Cisco was highlighted as Zacks' "Bear of the Day," citing "slowing growth, a cutthroat competitive landscape, and a weak industry group." On 2023-12-29, a separate article noted Cisco gained while the broader market dipped.
*   **Insider Activity (2023-12-19):** A news article noted that insiders at Cisco were among those selling shares, a potential signal of concern or, as the article cautions, a preplanned sale.

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks (Confirmed Evidence):**
*   **Slowing Growth & Competitive Pressure (2023-12-22):** The "Bear of the Day" analysis explicitly identifies "slowing growth" and a "cutthroat competitive landscape" as significant challenges.
*   **Legal and Regulatory Exposure (2023-12-11 & 2023-12-12):** The $8.6 million settlement over product vulnerabilities serves as a "warning" of potential future liability. While the Centripetal case was resolved favorably, the history of prolonged, high-stakes patent litigation is a demonstrated risk.
*   **Product Vulnerability & Reputational Risk (2023-12-11):** The settlement cited above highlights the risk of being held liable for security flaws in products, which could impact reputation and financials.
*   **Insider Selling (2023-12-19):** Noted as a potential negative signal regarding management's view of the company's prospects or valuation.

**Offsets (Confirmed Evidence):**
*   **Strong Recent Performance & Software Momentum (2023-11-20):** Q1 FY2024 results showed record performance and double-digit growth in key software and subscription metrics, indicating successful progress in its business model transformation.
*   **Strategic M&A for Growth (2023-12-04):** The Splunk acquisition is viewed by analysts as a positive long-term catalyst for software revenue growth.
*   **Favorable Legal Ruling (2023-12-13):** The court's rejection of Centripetal's infringement claims eliminates a major financial overhang.
*   **Strength in Key Themes (2023-11-15):** Management expressed confidence in growth fueled by AI, Security, Cloud, and Observability—areas central to its stated strategy.
*   **Financial Health (2023-11-27):** A qualitative note highlighted a "perfect Piotroski Score of 9," indicating strong fundamental financial health.

#### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral, with Positive Momentum Tempered by Structural Concerns.**
The evidence presents a mixed and nuanced picture. On the positive side, Cisco's latest operational results (Q1 FY2024) demonstrated strength and validated its strategic shift toward software and recurring revenue. The impending Splunk acquisition and resolutions of major legal risks provide clear, near-term catalysts. However, these positive developments are offset by credible, sourced commentary citing "slowing growth" and intense competition in its core markets, alongside ongoing legal and reputational risks related to product security. The juxtaposition of record earnings with a "Bear of the Day" designation and insider selling suggests the market remains divided on Cisco's ability to sustain growth in a challenging environment. Therefore, the qualitative posture is **Neutral**, with improvement in execution on software transition as the key variable for a positive shift.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Gross margin 65.2% (9.56B/14.67B), up from 61.3% YoY' and 'Operating margin 29.2% (4.28B/14.67B), up from 26.0% YoY' and 'Net margin 24.8% (3.64B/14.67B), up from 19.6% YoY.' The raw valuation ratios show gross_margin=0.637, operating_margin=0.272, and net_margin=0.234 for the latest period. The quant report's gross margin (65.2%) and operating margin (29.2%) are higher than the raw pack's figures (63.7% and 27.2%). The YoY comparisons (61.3%, 26.0%, 19.6%) are not directly verifiable from the raw packs since no prior-year margin ratios are provided. The 65.2% gross margin is arithmetically correct from the income statement (9.56/14.67=0.6517), but the raw valuation panel reports 0.637, suggesting a possible trailing or different-basis figure. The quant report should note this discrepancy or clarify which basis is used.
corrections.required_action: Flag that the margin figures derived from the latest quarterly income statement (65.2%, 29.2%, 24.8%) differ from the raw valuation panel's reported margins (63.7%, 27.2%, 23.4%), likely due to different calculation bases (single quarter vs. trailing). The YoY margin improvements cited (from 61.3%, 26.0%, 19.6%) cannot be verified from the supplied raw packs.

corrections.issue: Qual report states 'Q1 FY2024 results showed record performance' citing 2023-11-20 and 2023-11-15 sources. However, the raw quantitative trajectory shows Q1 FY24 revenue of $14.67B was below Q4 FY23's $15.20B, and Q1 FY24 EPS of $0.89 was below Q4 FY23's $0.96. The 'record' claim appears to come from management commentary in the earnings release (2023-11-15) and is attributed as such, but the quant report's own data shows QoQ declines. The qual report should more clearly attribute 'record' to management's characterization (likely YoY records) rather than presenting it as an unqualified fact.
corrections.required_action: Note that the 'record results' language is management's characterization from the earnings release and likely refers to YoY comparisons. The raw data shows Q1 FY24 was not a record on a quarterly basis (Q4 FY23 was higher on both revenue and EPS). This is a minor attribution issue but could mislead if read in isolation.

corrections.issue: The qual report mentions a 'perfect Piotroski Score of 9' from a 2023-11-27 source. This is a third-party analytical metric not directly verifiable from the raw financial data provided. While the raw packs do show strong balance sheet metrics (debt-to-equity 0.17, growing equity, positive earnings), the Piotroski Score itself is not computed or validated in the supplied evidence. This is acceptable as a cited external source but should not be treated as independently verified.
corrections.required_action: No action required; the Piotroski Score is properly attributed to an external source. Minor note for PM awareness that this is a third-party metric, not independently computed from the raw packs.

unsupported_claims: > The quant report's YoY margin comparisons ('up from 61.3% YoY' for gross margin, 'up from 26.0% YoY' for operating margin, 'up from 19.6% YoY' for net margin) cannot be verified from the raw packs, which do not provide prior-year margin figures. The raw valuation panel shows different current-period margins (63.7%, 27.2%, 23.4%) than the quant report's single-quarter derivations (65.2%, 29.2%, 24.8%), suggesting basis differences that are not explained.

> The qual report's statement that 'Q1 FY2024 results showed record performance' is sourced to management commentary but is not fully consistent with the raw quarterly trajectory data showing QoQ declines. The 'record' characterization is not independently verifiable from the supplied quantitative evidence.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Cisco presents a balanced risk/reward with attractive valuation (PE 15.2 at the low end of its range) and strong YoY fundamental growth (+37% EPS, expanding margins), but recent QoQ deceleration and lack of cash flow data temper conviction. The pending Splunk acquisition and software transition provide upside optionality, while slowing growth concerns and competitive pressure cap the near-term thesis.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Cisco presents a balanced risk/reward with attractive valuation (PE 15.2 at the low end of its range) and strong YoY fundamental growth (+37% EPS, expanding margins), but recent QoQ deceleration and lack of cash flow data temper conviction. The pending Splunk acquisition and software transition provide upside optionality, while slowing growth concerns and competitive pressure cap the near-term thesis.
key_factors: PE of 15.2 sits at the low end of its 2023 range (14.4–20.7), with historical average ~17.6, implying ~15% upside on multiple reversion alone if EPS holds at ~$3.56 annualized

YoY EPS growth of +36.9% and margin expansion (gross margin +390 bps YoY to 65.2%) demonstrate strong fundamental momentum, though QoQ declines (-3.5% revenue, -7.3% EPS) signal potential deceleration

Balance sheet fortress: debt-to-equity of 0.17 (down from 0.22 YoY), debt reduced from $8.9B to $7.7B, equity grew from $40.3B to $45.2B, providing financial flexibility for the $28B Splunk acquisition
Pending Splunk acquisition (announced 2023-12-04) expected to significantly expand software revenue and accelerate recurring revenue transition, though integration execution risk remains

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2023-01-27 section=business period_end=2022-12-31
Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-rotor aircraft; and commercial derivative aircraft, including anti-submarine and tanker aircraft. In addition, this segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of the following products and related services: strategic defense and intelligence systems, including strategic missile and defense systems, command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C4ISR), cyber and information solutions, and intelligence systems, satellite systems, including government and commercial satellites and space exploration.
Global Services Segment
This segment provides services to our commercial and defense customers worldwide. BGS sustains aerospace platforms and systems with a full spectrum of products and services, including supply chain and logistics management, engineering, maintenance and modifications, upgrades and conversions, spare parts, pilot and maintenance training systems and services, technical and maintenance documents, and data analytics and digital services.
Boeing Capital Segment
BCC seeks to ensure that Boeing customers have the financing they need to buy and take delivery of their Boeing product, while managing overall financing exposure. BCC's portfolio consists of equipment under operating leases, sal
…[truncated 7741 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4
Table of Contents
are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and similar expressions generally identify these forward-looking statements. Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact.

Table of Contents

**Quantitative Report: BA (Boeing) as of 2024-01-01**

**1. Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Q3 2023 revenue $18.10B, YoY +13.5% (from $15.96B), but QoQ -8.3% (from $19.75B). TTM revenue $75.76B.
- **Earnings**: Net loss Q3 2023 -$1.64B (EPS -$2.70). TTM net loss -$2.88B. YoY EPS -50.8% (from -$5.49 in Q3 2022, which included a large loss).
- **Margins**: Q3 2023 gross margin 6.5% ($1.17B / $18.10B), operating margin -4.5% (-$808M / $18.10B), net margin -9.1%. TTM gross margin 9.1% ($6.88B / $75.76B), operating margin -1.9% (-$1.41B / $75.76B), net margin -3.8%.
- **Cash-Flow**: No data available for any period.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total equity negative -$16.72B (Q3 2023). Total debt $52.03B, total assets $134.28B. Debt has decreased from $57.92B in Q4 2021.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Persistent negative net income over the last 8 quarters, with only one quarter of positive EPS (Q2 2022: $0.32). No cash-flow data to assess accruals or cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Negative equity indicates accumulated losses exceed assets. Debt-to-asset ratio is 38.7% ($52.03B / $134.28B). Cash position not available for recent quarters; last reported cash was $14.30B in Q3 2022.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- **P/S Ratio**: 2.088 (as of 2023-12-29). Net margin has improved from -13.9% (2023-01-03) to -3.8% (2023-12-29) but remains negative.
- **Other Ratios**: P/E, EV/EBIT, FCF yield, and P/B are not available due to negative earnings or equity.

**4. Price Context**
- **Price**: $260.66 on 2023-12-29, near 52-week high of $267.54 and above 52-week low of $176.25.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (since 2023-09-29) +36.0%, annual return (since 2022-12-28) +38.4%. Price appreciation contrasts with negative earnings trajectory.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside**: Revenue growth YoY (+13.5% in Q3 2023), improving net margin (from -13.9% to -3.8% over 2023), and debt reduction ($57.92B to $52.03B over two years). Strong price momentum (+36% quarterly).
- **Downside**: Negative equity (-$16.72B), high debt ($52.03B), and persistent losses (TTM net loss -$2.88B). No cash-flow visibility. Valuation (P/S 2.088) is elevated given negative net margin.

**6. Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- Quantitative evidence shows fundamental distress (negative equity, losses) despite recent price strength and margin improvement. The stock’s +38.4% annual return is disconnected from the negative earnings trajectory. Risk is high due to balance-sheet weakness and lack of cash-flow data; reward depends on achieving sustained profitability, which is not yet evident. Conditional on supplied evidence, risk/reward appears unfavorable.
BA.qualitative_report:
## Boeing (BA) — Qualitative Assessment Report
**Decision Date: 2024-01-01**

---

### 1. Business / Franchise Read

Boeing is one of the world's major aerospace firms, organized into four segments:
- **Commercial Airplanes (BCA):** Produces the 737 narrow-body and 767, 777, 787 wide-body jets; discontinued the 747 in 2022; development continues on the 777X and 737-7/-10 derivatives.
- **Defense, Space & Security (BDS):** Military aircraft, weapons systems, satellites, and space exploration for U.S. and allied governments; competes with Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, Raytheon, General Dynamics, and SpaceX.
- **Global Services (BGS):** Aftermarket services spanning maintenance, supply chain, training, and digital solutions for both commercial and defense customers.
- **Boeing Capital (BCC):** Customer financing arm managing leases, receivables, and related assets.

Boeing operates in a **duopoly with Airbus** in commercial aircraft (per 10-K competition section and web context 2023-12-09). The defense segment faces strong, ongoing competition from multiple primes and emerging entrants. The company is heavily dependent on global supply chains for aluminum, titanium, and composites, and on sole-source suppliers for major components. Workforce of ~156,000 (year-end 2022), with ~50,000 union members and significant collective bargaining agreements (earliest major IAM expiration: September 2024).

---

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated, from Supplied Text)

| Date | Development |
|---|---|
| **2023-12-30** | Boeing and Lockheed matched SpaceX pricing at their ULA joint-venture space launch subsidiary; reporting suggests this could *improve* profitability despite lower prices. |
| **2023-12-28** | Boeing urged airlines to inspect 737 Max jets for **possible loose bolts**, causing a ~1% stock drop — a direct quality/safety concern on its highest-volume program. |
| **2023-12-28** | SpaceX/IPO chatter in space launch; competitive context for Boeing's space activities via ULA via BDS. |
| **2023-12-27** | Boeing set to deliver **five 787 Dreamliners to LATAM Group**, signaling improved customer relationships in Latin America and continued wide-body delivery momentum. |
| **2023-12-09** | Web context notes Boeing's duopoly position and fleet-renewal tailwinds post-pandemic. |
| **2023-11-29** | RBC analyst upgrades BA to Street-high $275 target, citing **improving fundamentals**. |
| **2023-11-28** | Analyst describes Boeing's **2024 outlook as favorable**, with commercial and defense segments exhibiting sustainable growth. |
| **2023-12-13** | Seeking Alpha notes upside potential tied to **positive core operating income** trajectory and confirmed FY 2023 free cash flow outlook. |
| **2023-12-22** | AP News documents Boeing paid **$12 million in 2015** to settle FAA safety investigations — a historical regulatory data point with ongoing reputational relevance. |
| **2023-12-25** | Report of historical Boeing doctor warnings regarding **toxic workplace risks** — resurfacing legacy safety culture concerns. |
| **2023-12-01** | Ongoing litigation matter (*Sanders v. Boeing Company*). |

---

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets

**Risks:**
- **Safety / quality culture:** The 2023-12-28 737 Max bolt inspection alert (the Max being Boeing's cash-flow engine) echoes the post-MAX-crash safety narrative and suggests quality-control risks remain unresolved. The resurfacing of historical settlement and workplace safety documents (2023-12-22, 2023-12-25) compounds reputational exposure.
- **Regulatory / certification:** FAA and EASA certifications are prerequisites for new deliveries (777X, 737-7/-10); any delays directly impair revenue and cash flow. The 10-K explicitly notes heavy FAA regulation of commercial aircraft.
- **Supply chain / sole-source dependency:** Inability to qualify alternative suppliers quickly (12+ months for raw materials) and reliance on sole-source subcontractors creates production risk. Titanium sourcing shifted away from Russia in 2022 (10-K).
- **Labor relations:** The largest IAM contract expires **September 2024**, creating a near-term labor disruption risk on the critical commercial production lines.
- **Competitive pressure in space/defense:** SpaceX's pricing advantage and potential IPO of Starlink could further pressure ULA and Boeing's space launch relevance.
- **Ongoing litigation:** At least one active case (Sanders v. Boeing) and historical regulatory settlements suggest continued legal exposure.

**Offsets:**
- **Duopoly structure:** Airbus and Boeing together face record backlogs; an Airbus source (2023-12-26) projects the world needing **double the aircraft fleet by 2041**, providing durable demand visibility.
- **Improving fundamentals / analyst sentiment:** Multiple 2023-Q4 analyst notes cite favorable 2024 outlook, improving operating income, and confirmed free cash flow guidance (2023-11-28, 2023-11-29, 2023-12-13).
- **Commercial delivery momentum:** The LATAM 787 deal (2023-12-27) signals healthy wide-body demand and customer engagement recovery.
- **Space launch pricing:** Matching SpaceX pricing at ULA (2023-12-30) may actually improve margins, partially mitigating the competitive-erosion narrative.
- **Services annuity:** BGS provides recurring, higher-margin revenue that is less cyclical than new-aircraft deliveries.

---

### 4. Qualitative Assessment

**Verdict: Cautiously Positive / Neutral**

Boeing's franchise remains structurally strong via its commercial duopoly, growing services backlog, and defense diversification. The improving analyst outlook for 2024 and confirmed cash flow trajectory provide an encouraging near-term backdrop. However, the 737 Max bolt inspection event on 2023-12-28 — echoing prior safety failures on the same program — is a material qualitative concern that undermines investor confidence in Boeing's manufacturing discipline and quality culture. Combined with an IAM contract expiration in September 2024 and persistent regulatory/supply-chain tail risks, the positive catalysts are partially offset. The balance of evidence leans modestly constructive on franchise value but carries elevated execution and reputational risk through 2024.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'YoY EPS -50.8% (from -$5.49 in Q3 2022, which included a large loss)' — the parenthetical is misleading. The -50.8% figure is calculated as (-2.70 - (-5.49)) / |-5.49| = +50.8% improvement, not a -50.8% deterioration. The raw pack shows Q3 2022 EPS = -5.49 and Q3 2023 EPS = -2.70, so EPS improved YoY. The report's framing 'YoY EPS -50.8%' is ambiguous and could be read as a worsening; the sign convention should be clarified or the metric restated as 'EPS improved 50.8% YoY from -$5.49 to -$2.70'.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that EPS improved ~50% YoY (less negative), not deteriorated. The current phrasing risks misdirection on trajectory.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net margin has improved from -13.9% (2023-01-03) to -3.8% (2023-12-29)' — this is correct per the raw valuation ratios pack. However, the report does not note that the net margin improvement is largely driven by the Q2 2022 large loss (-$3.31B) rolling out of the TTM window, not by a fundamental step-change in quarterly profitability. Q3 2023 net margin was -9.1% (worse than TTM -3.8%), suggesting the TTM improvement is mechanical. This omission could overstate the sustainability of margin recovery.
corrections.required_action: Note that TTM margin improvement is partly mechanical (large loss quarter rolling off) and that Q3 2023 standalone margin (-9.1%) remains deeply negative.

corrections.issue: Qual report cites 'confirmed FY 2023 free cash flow outlook' (2023-12-13 Seeking Alpha) and 'improving operating income' as positive catalysts. The raw quant pack shows no cash-flow data for any period and TTM operating income of -$1.41B (negative). The qual report does not flag the absence of cash-flow verification or the negative TTM operating income, which partially contradicts the 'improving operating income' narrative without further qualification.
corrections.required_action: Flag that cash-flow data is absent from supplied evidence and that TTM operating income is negative (-$1.41B), so the 'confirmed FCF outlook' and 'improving operating income' claims cannot be independently verified from the raw packs.

unsupported_claims: : The qual report's 'confirmed outlook for FY 2023 free cash flow' (sourced from 2023-12-13 Seeking Alpha) cannot be verified — the raw quant pack contains no cash-flow data for any period, so this claim has no supporting evidence in the supplied packs.

The qual report's 'improving operating income trajectory' (2023-12-13 Seeking Alpha) is not fully supported by the raw pack: TTM operating income is -$1.41B and Q3 2023 operating income is -$808M. While Q3 is less negative than Q2 2022's -$2.80B, the trajectory is not unambiguously 'improving' on a quarterly basis (Q2 2023 was -$99M, Q3 2023 worsened to -$808M). The claim should be qualified.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing's duopoly franchise and improving revenue trajectory provide a structural floor, but persistent negative equity (-$16.72B), ongoing losses (TTM net loss -$2.88B), and recent quality concerns (737 Max bolt inspection alert on 2023-12-28) keep risk/reward roughly balanced. The stock's +38.4% annual return has already priced in much of the turnaround narrative, leaving limited upside absent a clear path to sustained profitability.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing's duopoly franchise and improving revenue trajectory provide a structural floor, but persistent negative equity (-$16.72B), ongoing losses (TTM net loss -$2.88B), and recent quality concerns (737 Max bolt inspection alert on 2023-12-28) keep risk/reward roughly balanced. The stock's +38.4% annual return has already priced in much of the turnaround narrative, leaving limited upside absent a clear path to sustained profitability.
key_factors: Revenue growth remains solid but decelerating: TTM revenue $75.76B with Q3 2023 YoY growth of +13.5%, though QoQ declined -8.3% from $19.75B to $18.10B, suggesting the easy recovery phase may be maturing.

Balance sheet remains structurally impaired: negative equity of -$16.72B, total debt of $52.03B, and TTM net loss of -$2.88B. While debt has decreased from $57.92B in Q4 2021, the company has not achieved sustained profitability — only one positive EPS quarter in the last eight.

TTM margin improvement is partly mechanical: net margin improved from -13.9% to -3.8% over 2023, but this is largely driven by the Q2 2022 large loss (-$3.31B) rolling off the TTM window. Q3 2023 standalone net margin was -9.1%, indicating the underlying quarterly profitability has not fundamentally improved.

Stock has already rallied +38.4% over the past year to $260.66 (near 52-week high of $267.54), pricing in much of the turnaround narrative. P/S of 2.088 is elevated given negative net margins, and the IAM contract expiration in September 2024 plus the 737 Max quality concerns create near-term execution risk that could pressure the multiple.

ITEM 1.    Business
OVERVIEW
Visa is one of the world's leaders in digital payments. Our purpose is to uplift everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid. We facilitate global commerce and money movement across more than 200 countries and territories among a global set of consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities through innovative technologies.
Since Visa's early days in 1958, we have been in the business of facilitating payments between consumers and businesses. We are focused on extending, enhancing and investing in our proprietary advanced transaction processing network, VisaNet, to offer a single connection point for facilitating payment transactions to multiple endpoints through various form factors. As a network of networks enabling global movement of money through all available networks, we are working to provide payment solutions and services for everyone, everywhere. Through our network, we offer products, solutions and services that facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement for participants in the ecosystem.
- We facilitate secure, reliable and efficient money movement among consumers, issuing and acquiring financial institutions and merchants. We have traditionally referred to this structure as the "four-party" model. Please see Our Core Business discussion below. As the payments ecosystem continues to evolve, we have broadened this model to include digital banks, digital wallets and a range of financial technology companies (fintechs), governments and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). We provide transaction processing services (primarily authorization, clearing and settlement) to our financial institution and merchant clients through VisaNet. During fiscal year 2023, 276 billion payments and cash transactions with Visa's brand were processed by Visa or other networks, equating to an average of 757 million transactions per day. Of the 276 billion total transactions, 213 billion were processed by Visa.
- We offer a wide range of Visa-branded payment products that our clients, including 14,500 financial institutions, use to develop and offer payment solutions or services, including credit, debit, prepaid and cash access programs for individual, business and government account holders. During fiscal year 2023, Visa's total payments and cash volume was $15 trillion, and 4.3 billion payment credentials, which are issued Visa card accounts that were available worldwide to be used at more than 130 million merchant locations.(1)
- We take an open partnership approach and seek to provide value by enabling access to our global network, including offering our technology capabilities through application programming interfaces (APIs). We partner with b
…[truncated 19253 chars]…
anager, Visa Consumer Authentication Service, and payment-decisioning solutions from CardinalCommerce empower financial institutions and merchants with tools that help automate and simplify fraud prevention and enhance payment security.
Aligned to our network of networks strategy, Visa is increasingly bringing our expertise and capabilities to emerging fraud challenges, working with network operators and financial institutions to help mitigate fraud. These value-added fraud prevention tools layer on top of a suite of our network programs that protect the safety and integrity of the payment ecosystem, and along with our investments in intelligence and technology, help to prevent, detect and mitigate threats. These programs and Visa's fraud prevention expertise are among the core benefits of
10
Table of Contents
being part of the Visa network. Through the combined efforts of security and identity tools and services, payment and cyber intelligence, insights and learnings from client or partner breach investigations, and law enforcement engagement, Visa helps protect financial institutions and merchants from fraud and solve payment security challenges.
Open Banking
In March 2022, Visa acquired Tink AB, an open banking platform, to catalyze fintech innovation and accelerate the development and adoption of open banking securely and at scale. Visa's open banking capabilities range from data access use cases, such as account verification, balance check and personal finance management, to payment initiation capabilities, such as account-to-account transactions and merchant payments. These capabilities can help our partner businesses deliver valuable services to their customers.
Advisory Services
Visa Consulting and Analytics (VCA) is the payments consulting advisory arm of Visa. The combination of our deep payments expertise, proprietary analytical models applied to a breadth of data and our economic intelligence allows us to identify actionable insights, make recommendations and help implement solutions that can drive better business decisions and measurable outcomes for clients. VCA offers consulting services for issuers, acquirers, merchants, fintechs and other partners, spanning the entire customer journey from acquisition to retention. Further, VCA Managed Services, our dedicated execution arm within the consulting division, is being increasingly utilized by clients to implement our recommendations and wider value added services product enablement.

We are fortifying the key foundations of our business model, which consist of becoming a network of networks, our technology platforms, security, brand and talent.

Network of Networks
Our network of networks strategy means moving money to all endpoints and to all form factors, using all available networks and being a single connection point for our partners; and providing our value added services on all transactions, no matter the network. The key component of our network of networks strategy is interoperability. We are opening up our network and increasingly using other networks to reach accounts we could not otherwise reach and enabling new types of money movement. Visa B2B Connect, Visa Direct, and Visa+ are examples of our strategy.
Technology Platforms
Visa's leading technology platforms comprise software, hardware, data centers and a large telecommunications infrastructure. Visa's four data centers are a critical part of our global processing environment and have a high redundancy of network connectivity, power and cooling designed to provide continuous availability of systems. Together, these systems deliver the secure, convenient and reliable service that our clients and consumers expect from the Visa brand.
Security
Our in-depth, multi-layer security approach includes a formal program to devalue sensitive and/or personal data through various cryptographic means; embedded security in the software development lifecycle; identity and access management controls to protect against unauthorized access; and advanced cyber detection and response capabilities. We deploy security tools that help keep our clients and consumers safe. We also invest significantly in our comprehensive approach to cybersecurity. We deploy security technologies to protect data confidentiality, the integrity of our network and service availability to strengthen our core cybersecurity capabilities to minimize risk. Our
11
Table of Contents
payments fraud disruption team continually monitors threats to the payments ecosystem to help ensure attacks are detected and prevented efficiently and effectively.
Brand
Visa's strong brand helps deliver added value to our clients and their customers, financial institutions, merchants and partners through compelling brand expressions, a wide range of products and services as well as innovative brand and marketing efforts. In line with our commitment to an expansive and diverse range of partnerships for the benefit of our stakeholders, Visa is a sponsor of top entertainment and sports events including the FIFA Women's World Cup 2023TM, the Olympic and Paralympic Games, and the Super Bowl.
Talent
Attracting, developing and advancing the best talent globally is critical to our continued success. This year we grew our total workforce from approximately 26,500 in fiscal year 2022 to approximately 28,800 employees in fiscal year 2023, an increase of 9 percent year over year. Voluntary workforce turnover (rolling 12-month attrition) was 6 percent as of September 30, 2023. Visa employees are located in more than 80 countries and territories, with 55 percent located outside the U.S. At the end of fiscal year 2023, Visa's global workforce was 58 percent men and 42 percent women, and women represented 36 percent of Visa's leadership (defined as vice president level and above). In the U.S., ethnicity of our workforce was 42 percent Asian, 8 percent Black, 13 percent Hispanic, 3 percent Other and 35 percent White. For our U.S. leadership, the breakdown was 18 percent Asian, 6 percent Black, 13 percent Hispanic, 3 percent Other and 60 percent White.
Given Visa's ambitious growth agenda and efforts to achieve our purpose, we have focused on enhancing our employees' expertise across our business. This includes an enhanced development program for our senior leaders and a formal technology apprenticeship program to help us broaden and strengthen our talent channels and pipelines. We have also committed to providing employees with the tools they need to do their work more quickly and easily, including an artificial intelligence or AI-driven portal with a searchable knowledge base to create customized results and bespoke solutions. We enhanced our mental well-being and retirement benefits, which is reflective of our key priority to take care of our employees.
We also are dedicated to ensuring that employees feel valued in their day-to-day work. During our global employee engagement survey last year, we learned that our employees wanted more opportunities to recognize and be recognized, in more informal ways. In response, Visa developed a program that better enabled employees to provide peer-to-peer recognition for each other's contributions. Using UPLIFT, Visa's new recognition platform, employees can celebrate their peers' achievements, send e-cards to celebrate the employee journey (from welcoming new hires to recognizing service anniversaries), use an automated internal networking tool that matches employees based on smart algorithms, and more. Importantly, all our recognition categories are grounded in behaviors that reflect our employee value proposition or Visa's Leadership Principles – further reinforcing that at Visa, it is not only about what you achieve, but how you do it. Employee engagement in peer recognition has significantly increased since the launch, with monthly active users reaching 78 percent in September 2023, compared to 45 percent in September 2022

**Quantitative Report: Visa Inc. (V) as of 2024-01-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue:** Consistent quarterly growth from Q1 2021 ($7.06B) to Q4 2023 ($8.61B). Latest QoQ growth: +6.0%; YoY growth: +10.6%. TTM revenue: $32.65B.
- **Earnings:** Net income grew from Q1 2021 ($3.96B) to Q4 2023 ($4.68B). YoY net income growth: +18.8%. TTM net income: $17.27B; TTM operating income: $21.00B.
- **Margins:** TTM net margin: 52.9%; TTM operating margin: 64.3%. Quarterly net margin (Q4 2023): 54.4% ($4.68B/$8.61B). Margins stable (historical range: 50.0%–52.9%).
- **Cash Flow:** Unavailable for all periods.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total assets increased from $81.93B (Q1 2021) to $90.50B (Q4 2023). Total equity grew from $36.19B to $38.73B. Total liabilities: $51.77B (latest). Leverage (total liabilities/equity): 1.34x. Total debt not reported after Q1 2021 ($20.92B).

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** High and stable TTM net margin (52.9%) and operating margin (64.3%) suggest strong profitability. TTM ROE: 44.6%. However, absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of accruals or cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Equity positive and growing slowly. Leverage ratio (1.34x) moderate. Without recent debt figures or interest coverage, resilience cannot be fully evaluated.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- Traditional valuation multiples (P/E, EV/EBIT, FCF yield, P/B) are unavailable. Margins and ROE are stable: net margin ranged 50.0%–52.9%, ROE ranged 40.9%–44.6% over the provided 12-point history. High profitability is consistent, but no multiple-based valuation is possible.

**4. Price Context vs. Fundamentals**
- **Price Level:** $260.35 (2023-12-29), near 52-week high ($263.25).
- **Returns:** Annual return (from $204.99): +27.0%. Quarterly return (from $230.01): +13.2%.
- **Fundamental Path:** Annual revenue growth (+10.6%) and net income growth (+18.8%) trailed stock appreciation, indicating multiple expansion over the period.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Consistent revenue and earnings growth (TTM net income $17.27B), high margins (net margin 52.9%), and robust ROE (44.6%). If growth persists, further appreciation is possible.
- **Downside:** Absence of cash flow data obscures earnings quality. Price near 52-week high with recent returns exceeding fundamental growth. Balance sheet liabilities rising ($51.77B vs. $45.73B in Q1 2021) without debt detail poses unquantified risk.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Fundamentals show strong growth and profitability, but valuation cannot be assessed due to missing multiples and cash flow data. Price performance has outpaced earnings growth, suggesting the market has priced in future expansion. Without valuation anchors, risk/reward is indeterminate from the supplied numbers. Key quantitative gap: no cash flow metrics or current debt levels.
V.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis Report: Visa Inc. (V)**
**Decision Date:** 2024-01-01
**Evidence Window:** Point-in-time data through 2023-12-31

### 1. Business / Franchise Read from Narrative
Based on its 2023-11-15 10-K filing, Visa operates a leading global digital payments network (VisaNet) facilitating transactions across more than 200 countries. It operates a "four-party" model, connecting consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and governments, and has expanded to include fintechs and digital wallets. Visa is not a bank and does not issue cards or bear credit risk; it earns revenue through service, data processing, and international transaction fees. Its core strategy is to accelerate growth in three pillars: **Consumer Payments** (migrating cash/checks to cards and digital), **New Flows** (e.g., person-to-person, business-to-business via Visa Direct and Visa B2B Connect), and **Value Added Services** (issuing, acceptance, risk, identity, and advisory services). The company emphasizes its "network of networks" strategy, technological platform, security, and strong brand.

### 2. Recent Developments (from supplied text, dated)
*   **Growth & Market Sentiment (2023-12-27 to 2023-12-31):** Multiple news articles highlight Visa as a recommended "buy" for dividend growth and market performance, citing its potential to reach a $1 trillion valuation by 2030. The stock's 25.6% gain over the past year was noted, with "revenge travel" seen as a catalyst for cross-border payment growth (2023-12-27, 2023-12-30).
*   **Sector Outlook (2023-12-29):** A report stated payment stocks like Visa are expected to benefit from resilient consumer spending and a stable labor market, though it cautioned that regulatory challenges and high costs could hamper results.
*   **Capital Allocation (2023-12-30):** A web analysis noted Visa's 0.8% dividend yield and historical share repurchase activity (~2.4% in 2023), framing them as components of shareholder return.
*   **Strategic Position (2023-11-16):** A SWOT analysis cited Visa's acquisition of Tink AB (an open banking platform) as a move to capitalize on digital payments innovation.
*   **Governance & Litigation (2023-11-22):** Visa's board approved a shareholder vote on a plan to address how the company covers billions in outstanding merchant litigation liability through a share exchange program.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risk: Regulatory & Legal Pressure.** The 10-K (2023-11-15) states that regulators worldwide are increasingly taking note of each other's approaches to payments, which adds cost and impacts strategy. A specific litigation liability exists from merchant claims, leading to the proposed share exchange program (2023-11-22). News also flags regulatory challenges as a sector headwind (2023-12-29).
*   **Offset: Strong Competitive Moat & Scale.** The franchise is built on a vast, global, high-transaction network (276B transactions in FY23) with significant barriers to entry. Its strategy of becoming a "network of networks" and strong brand, supported by major sponsorships (e.g., Olympics, NFL), fortifies its position (10-K, 2023-11-15).
*   **Risk: Dependence on Consumer & Macroeconomic Health.** Revenue is tied to payment volume, which is sensitive to consumer spending, employment, and travel trends. A downturn could impact growth (implied by 10-K business model; news, 2023-12-29).
*   **Offset: Diversification into New Flows & Services.** Growth is not solely dependent on consumer-to-business payments. Expansion into commercial (B2B) and peer-to-peer (P2P) money movement via Visa Direct and value-added services provides alternative revenue streams (10-K, 2023-11-15).

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.**

The supplied narrative presents Visa as a high-quality franchise with a durable competitive advantage rooted in its global network, scale, and trusted brand. Recent news sentiment is bullish, highlighting sustainable growth drivers like the secular shift to digital payments and cross-border travel. While material regulatory and litigation risks are confirmed in the evidence, the company's strategic initiatives in new payment flows and value-added services, coupled with its fundamental strengths, suggest a resilient business model capable of navigating these challenges.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Visa remains an elite franchise with durable double-digit revenue growth (+10.6% YoY) and best-in-class profitability (net margin 52.9%, ROE 44.6%), but the stock's +27% annual return has outpaced fundamental growth, leaving it near its 52-week high with limited valuation upside. Regulatory and litigation overhangs, including the merchant liability share exchange program, further temper the risk/reward.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Visa remains an elite franchise with durable double-digit revenue growth (+10.6% YoY) and best-in-class profitability (net margin 52.9%, ROE 44.6%), but the stock's +27% annual return has outpaced fundamental growth, leaving it near its 52-week high with limited valuation upside. Regulatory and litigation overhangs, including the merchant liability share exchange program, further temper the risk/reward.
key_factors: TTM revenue of $32.65B with YoY growth of +10.6% and net income growth of +18.8%, demonstrating durable double-digit earnings expansion on a $17.27B net income base.
Net margin stable at 52.9% (range 50.3%–52.9%) and ROE at 44.6%, reflecting exceptional capital efficiency and pricing power that underpins the franchise moat.
Stock price of $260.35 near 52-week high ($263.25) with +27% annual return exceeding revenue growth, indicating multiple expansion that limits near-term upside without valuation anchors.

Regulatory and litigation risks remain material: the 10-K (2023-11-15) notes increasing global regulatory scrutiny, and a shareholder vote on a share exchange program to cover merchant litigation liability was approved (2023-11-22).

Item 1
Note About Forward-Looking Statements
This report includes estimates, projections, statements relating to our business plans, objectives, and expected operating results that are "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. Forward-looking statements may appear throughout this report, including the following sections: "Business" (Part I, Item 1 of this Form 10-K), "Risk Factors" (Part I, Item 1A of this Form 10-K), and "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations" (Part II, Item 7 of this Form 10-K). These forward-looking statements generally are identified by the words "believe," "project," "expect," "anticipate," "estimate," "intend," "strategy," "future," "opportunity," "plan," "may," "should," "will," "would," "will be," "will continue," "will likely result," and similar expressions. Forward-looking statements are based on current expectations and assumptions that are subject to risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially. We describe risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results and events to differ materially in "Risk Factors," "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations," and "Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures about Market Risk" (Part II, Item 7A of this Form 10-K). Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements, which speak only as of the date they are made. We undertake no obligation to update or revise publicly any forward-looking statements, whether because of new information, future events, or otherwise.
3
Item 1
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
Embracing Our Future
Microsoft is a technology company whose mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. We strive to create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world. We are creating the platforms and tools, powered by artificial intelligence ("AI"), that deliver better, faster, and more effective solutions to support small and large business competitiveness, improve educational and health outcomes, grow public-sector efficiency,  and empower human ingenuity. From infrastructure and data, to business applications and collaboration, we provide unique, differentiated value to customers.
In a world of increasing economic complexity, AI has the power to revolutionize many types of work. Microsoft is now innovating and expanding our portfolio with AI capabilities to help people and organizations overcome today's challenges and emerge stronger. Customers are looking to unlock value from their digital spen
…[truncated 19224 chars]…
ing, and inclusive work environment that enables our global employees to create products and services that further our mission.
8
Item 1
As of June 30, 2023, we employed approximately 221,000 people on a full-time basis, 120,000 in the U.S. and 101,000 internationally. Of the total employed people, 89,000 were in operations, including manufacturing, distribution, product support, and consulting services; 72,000 were in product research and development; 45,000 were in sales and marketing; and 15,000 were in general and administration. Certain employees are subject to collective bargaining agreements.
Our Culture
Microsoft's culture is grounded in growth mindset. This means everyone is on a continuous journey to learn and grow, operating as one company instead of multiple siloed businesses.
Our employee listening systems enable us to gather feedback directly from our workforce to inform our programs and employee needs globally. Employees participate in our Employee Signals surveys, which cover a variety of topics such as thriving, inclusion, team culture, wellbeing, and learning and development. We also collect Daily Signals employee survey responses, giving us real-time insights into ways we can support our employees. In addition to Employee Signals and Daily Signals surveys, we gain insights through onboarding, exit surveys, internal Viva Engage channels, employee Q&A sessions, and our internal AskHR Service support.
Diversity and inclusion are core to our business model, and we hold ourselves accountable for driving global systemic change in our workforce and creating an inclusive work environment. We support multiple highly active Employee Resource Groups for women, families, racial and ethnic minorities, military, people with disabilities, and employees who identify as LGBTQIA+, where employees can go for support, networking, and community-building. As described in our 2022 Proxy Statement, annual performance and compensation reviews of our senior leadership team include an evaluation of their contributions to employee culture and diversity. To ensure accountability over time, we publicly disclose our progress on a multitude of workforce metrics including:
- Detailed breakdowns of gender, racial, and ethnic minority representation in our employee population, with data by job types, levels, and segments of our business.
- Our EEO-1 report (equal employment opportunity).
- Disability representation.
- Pay equity (see details below).
Total Rewards and Pay Equity
We develop dynamic, sustainable, market-driven, and strategic programs with the goal of providing a highly differentiated portfolio to attract, reward, and retain top talent and enable our employees to thrive. These programs reinforce our culture and values such as collaboration and growth mindset. Managers evaluate and recommend rewards based on, for example, how well we leverage the work of others and contribute to the success of our colleagues. We monitor pay equity and career progress across multiple dimensions. Our total compensation opportunity is highly differentiated and is market competitive.
In order to manage our costs in a dynamic, competitive environment, in fiscal year 2023 we announced that base salaries of salaried employees would remain at fiscal year 2022 levels. Pay increases continue to be available for rewards-eligible hourly and equivalent employees. We will continue our practice of investing in stock for all rewards-eligible employees, salaried and hourly, and investing in bonuses for all eligible employees.
9
Item 1
Since 2016, we have reported on pay equity as part of our annual Diversity and Inclusion report. In 2022, we reported that all racial and ethnic minority employees in the U.S. combined earn $1.008 for every $1.000 earned by their white counterparts, that women in the U.S. earn $1.007 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts who are men, and that women outside the U.S. earn $1.002 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts outside the U.S. who are men. In this year's report, we again expanded our pay equity data beyond the U.S. to report on 61 additional countries (up from 12 last year), representing 99.8% of our global Microsoft workforce.
In addition, we began reporting on unadjusted median pay in our annual report, comparing total pay amounts for all employees regardless of factors such as job title, level, or tenure. For employees who are eligible for rewards, the analysis showed that total pay for women is 89.6% of total pay for men in the U.S. and 86.2% outside of the U.S., and total pay for racial and ethnic minorities in the U.S. is 89.9% of total pay for white employees. As we continue to increase representation for women and racial and ethnic minorities at more senior levels, and continue to ensure pay equity for all, the gap between the medians will reduce.
Our intended result is a global performance and development approach that fosters our culture, and competitive compensation that ensures equitable pay by role while supporting pay for performance.
Wellbeing and Hybrid Work
Microsoft is committed to supporting our employees' wellbeing while they are at work and in their personal lives. We have invested significantly in wellbeing, and offer a differentiated benefits package which includes many physical, emotional, and financial wellness programs including counseling through the Microsoft CARES Employee Assistance Program, mental wellbeing support, flexible fitness benefits, disability accommodations, savings and investment tools, adoption assistance, and back-up care for children and elders. Finally, our Occupational Health and Safety program helps ensure employees can stay safe while they are working.
We introduced Hybrid Workplace Flexibility Guidance to better support leaders, managers, and employees in hybrid work scenarios. Our ongoing survey data shows that 93% of employees value the flexibility related to work location, work site, and work hours, and 78% are satisfied with the quality of connection with co-workers. There is no one-size-fits-all approach to flexible work at Microsoft. As a company, we will continue to leverage data and research to inform decision making, balancing the needs of business, team, and individual.
Learning and Development
We offer a range of learning opportunities, including personalized opportunities on our internal and external learning portals, in-classroom learning, required learning on compliance and company culture, on-the-job advancement opportunities, and manager coaching. We also provide customized manager learning, new employee orientation, and tools for operating in a flexible hybrid work environment.
All Microsoft employees globally access our single Viva Learning tool for both required and personal choice learning. This includes courses focused on our core principles and compliance matters, such as Business Conduct, Privacy, Security Foundations, and Harassment Prevention. We also deliver skills training for employees based on their profession and role discipline.
We have over 27,000 people managers, all of whom must complete between 20-33 hours of compulsory training on leadership and management and are assigned additional targeted training on an ongoing basis related to people management, compliance, and culture.
10
Item 1
OPERATING SEGMENTS
We operate our business and report our financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. Our segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of our key businesses. The segments enable the alignment of strategies and objectives across the development, sales, marketing, and services organizations, and they provide a framework for timely and rational allocation of resources within businesses.
Additional information on our operating segments and geographic and product information is contained in Note 19 – Segment Info
